_note: Grounded disease -> management lookup. Active ingredients and cultural controls are drawn from ICAR / State Agricultural University extension advisories and Krishi Vigyan Kendra guides. Dosages are indicative ranges only; the label on the purchased product and local KVK advice always override this table. This file is the deterministic source of truth the LLM summarizer is allowed to read from — it must never invent an entry not present here.
_disclaimer: Confirm with your local Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) or agri-input dealer before applying any chemical. Follow the product label for exact dosage, pre-harvest interval, and safety precautions.

Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.label: Bacterial spot
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.indicative_dosage: Copper oxychloride 3 g/L water; add streptocycline 0.5 g/L as needed

Potato___Early_blight.label: Early blight
Potato___Early_blight.indicative_dosage: Mancozeb 2-2.5 g/L water, spray at 10-12 day intervals

Potato___Late_blight.label: Late blight
Potato___Late_blight.indicative_dosage: Metalaxyl+Mancozeb 2.5 g/L at first appearance; rotate with contact fungicide
cultural_controls: Spray preventively in cool humid weather

Tomato_Late_blight.label: Late blight
Tomato_Late_blight.indicative_dosage: 2.5 g/L at first symptoms in cool humid weather

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.label: Leaf mold
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.indicative_dosage: 2-2.5 g/L, focus on lower leaf surface

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.label: Two-spotted spider mite
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.indicative_dosage: Spiromesifen 1 mL/L; sulphur 2-3 g/L (not in hot weather)

Tomato__Target_Spot.label: Target spot
Tomato__Target_Spot.indicative_dosage: Azoxystrobin 1 mL/L; rotate MoA to avoid resistance

Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.label: Tomato yellow leaf curl virus (TYLCV)
active_ingredients: No chemical cures the virus. Manage whitefly vector: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL or Diafenthiuron 50% WP
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.indicative_dosage: Imidacloprid 0.3 mL/L for whitefly; rogue infected plants

chilli_anthracnose.label: Chilli anthracnose / fruit rot (die-back)
chilli_anthracnose.indicative_dosage: Difenoconazole 0.5 mL/L or Mancozeb 2.5 g/L at flowering/fruiting

chilli_leaf_curl_virus.label: Chilli leaf curl (viral complex, thrips/whitefly/mite-transmitted)
active_ingredients: Manage vectors: Fipronil 5% SC (thrips), Imidacloprid 17.8% SL (whitefly), Spiromesifen (mite)
chilli_leaf_curl_virus.indicative_dosage: Rotate insecticides; rogue severely infected plants

chilli_powdery_mildew.label: Chilli powdery mildew
chilli_powdery_mildew.indicative_dosage: Wettable sulphur 2-3 g/L; avoid sulphur in high heat

corn_northern_leaf_blight.label: Northern (turcicum) leaf blight of maize
corn_northern_leaf_blight.indicative_dosage: Mancozeb 2.5 g/L or Propiconazole 1 mL/L, 2-3 sprays

corn_gray_leaf_spot.label: Gray leaf spot of maize
corn_gray_leaf_spot.indicative_dosage: Azoxystrobin 1 mL/L at early disease onset

paddy_blast.label: Rice blast
paddy_blast.indicative_dosage: Tricyclazole 0.6 g/L at boot-leaf and heading

paddy_bacterial_leaf_blight.label: Bacterial leaf blight (BLB)
cultural_controls: Drain field, avoid flooding after infection

paddy_sheath_blight.label: Sheath blight of rice
paddy_sheath_blight.indicative_dosage: Hexaconazole 2 mL/L directed to plant base/sheath
cultural_controls: Avoid dense planting and excess N

toor_fusarium_wilt.label: Fusarium wilt of pigeon pea
active_ingredients: Seed treatment: Trichoderma viride / Carbendazim 50% WP
toor_fusarium_wilt.indicative_dosage: Seed treat Carbendazim 2 g/kg seed or Trichoderma 4 g/kg

toor_sterility_mosaic.label: Sterility mosaic disease (SMD)
active_ingredients: Manage mite vector: Dicofol 18.5% EC or wettable sulphur
toor_sterility_mosaic.indicative_dosage: Rogue infected 'bushy green' plants; control mite vector early

toor_phytophthora_blight.label: Phytophthora stem blight of pigeon pea

groundnut_tikka_leaf_spot.label: Tikka (early & late) leaf spot of groundnut
groundnut_tikka_leaf_spot.indicative_dosage: Chlorothalonil 2 g/L or Tebuconazole 1 mL/L, 2-3 sprays from 30 DAS

groundnut_rust.label: Groundnut rust
groundnut_rust.indicative_dosage: Sulphur 3 g/L or Tebuconazole 1 mL/L; often tank-mixed with leaf spot spray

groundnut_bud_necrosis.label: Groundnut bud necrosis disease (GBND)
active_ingredients: Manage thrips vector: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL / Fipronil 5% SC
groundnut_bud_necrosis.indicative_dosage: Seed treat Imidacloprid; early thrips control critical

cotton_bacterial_blight.label: Bacterial blight (angular leaf spot) of cotton

cotton_leaf_curl_virus.label: Cotton leaf curl virus (CLCuV)
active_ingredients: Manage whitefly: Diafenthiuron 50% WP / Flonicamid 50% WG
cotton_leaf_curl_virus.indicative_dosage: Rotate insecticides for whitefly; rogue infected plants early

cotton_root_rot.label: Root rot of cotton
active_ingredients: Seed treatment: Carbendazim 50% WP / Trichoderma viride
cotton_root_rot.indicative_dosage: Seed treat Carbendazim 2 g/kg; spot drench 1 g/L
